summary: Iter-13 — Resend domain-verified email-delivery verification + milestone flywheel re-verification. All 11/11 tests pass against the production preview URL. CONFIRMED via grep on /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log: (1) welcome email on /api/auth/register logs `resend.send ok id=<uuid> to=qa+<rand>@alreadyherellc.com subject=Welcome to TokenForge — your API key is ready` — NO sandbox rejection. (2) $1 milestone flow: seed $1.50 cost → /api/proxy/chat → backend writes `milestone_alerts` row with `key=<uid>:milestone:1` + auto-creates `share_links` row with `auto_created=true` + logs `resend.send ok ... subject=[TokenForge] You just saved $1 — share your receipt 🎉`. (3) Idempotency: second /api/proxy/chat call does NOT create a duplicate milestone_alerts row. (4) Static endpoints: /api/reports/savings.pdf returns valid PDF magic bytes %PDF, /api/share/savings/<slug>/og.png returns valid PNG magic bytes (placeholder fallback for unknown slug), /api/widget.js returns javascript content. (5) Regression spine: login, /api/auth/me, /api/keys, /api/dashboard/logs, /api/dashboard/overview all 200.

minor.issue: Resend API key (`re_BVhN3EAa_*`) STILL appears to be in TEST mode at the account level. With domain `alreadyherellc.com` verified, sends FROM `dispatch@alreadyherellc.com` TO `@alreadyherellc.com` recipients succeed (`resend.send ok`). But sends to non-verified-domain recipients (e.g., `test_xx@example.com`, `qa+xx@qa.tokenforge.io`) are STILL rejected with `resend.send rejected (sandbox): You can only send testing emails to your own email address (alreadyherellc@gmail.com). To send emails to other recipients, please verify a domain at resend.com/domains, and change the from address to an email using this domain.` The handoff note claimed 'domain is now verified so emails to any address should land' — that is only TRUE for recipients ON the verified domain. To enable arbitrary-recipient delivery, the user needs a LIVE Resend API key (test-mode keys are sandbox-restricted regardless of domain verification) OR confirm full domain verification status at resend.com/domains. NOT a TokenForge code bug — purely a Resend account configuration item.

action_items: (Optional infra) To unlock email delivery to ANY recipient (not just @alreadyherellc.com), either switch RESEND_API_KEY to a LIVE/production key or verify at resend.com/domains that `alreadyherellc.com` is fully verified (not pending DNS). The TokenForge app code is correct and ready — gated only by Resend account state.

(Optional UX) When Resend sandbox-rejects a send, the backend logs WARNING but the user-facing flow proceeds normally (best-effort email). Consider surfacing a banner on /dashboard for admin if recent_send_rejected_count > 0 in the last hour, so the operator notices when delivery silently fails for non-verified recipients.
critical_code_review_comments: email_service.py is correctly fail-open: rejected sends log a WARNING and return None, never raising. Milestone_alerts row is committed to MongoDB BEFORE the email send attempt, so re-fire never happens even if Resend rejects. This is the correct ordering (delivery is best-effort, dedupe is the source of truth).
Milestone trigger in server.py fires only the HIGHEST unseen tier per request (correct, anti-spam). Confirmed in iter-12 test suite. Verified again here with $1 seed firing only the $1 tier.

Auto-share-link creation reads `db.share_links.find_one({user_id})` then inserts if missing. Under heavy concurrency two parallel proxy_chat calls could both insert. Low risk because milestone_alerts has a unique key index acting as the actual dedupe, but a unique index on share_links.user_id is still recommended as defense-in-depth (already flagged in iter-12 report).
Resend API key `re_BVhN3EAa_*` is hardcoded in /app/backend/.env. Confirm key is rotated post-launch since it's been live in this preview env across 13 iterations.
SENDER_EMAIL format `TokenForge <dispatch@alreadyherellc.com>` is parsed correctly by the Resend SDK — verified via successful sends in logs.
test_credentials: admin@tokenforge.io / ForgeAdmin!2026 (per /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Test users registered ad-hoc as qa+<scenario>_<rand>@alreadyherellc.com (verified-domain recipients to guarantee Resend delivery).
seed_data_creation: Inserted proxy_requests rows directly into Mongo to seed cost_saved_usd=$1.50 for milestone trigger tests. Did NOT clean up — these linger in db.proxy_requests across runs (consistent with iter-12 behavior).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-13 confirms email delivery works for recipients on the verified domain `alreadyherellc.com`. Resend test-mode still blocks arbitrary-domain recipients — this is an account-level state, not a code bug. Future iterations should: (a) use `qa+<x>@alreadyherellc.com` recipients to get real resend.send ok confirmation; (b) accept that `@example.com` / `@qa.tokenforge.io` recipients will log `resend.send rejected (sandbox)` and assert the milestone_alerts/share_links DB-side effects still happen regardless (email is best-effort). Run iter-13 suite: `pytest backend/tests/test_tokenforge_iter13_email.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_iterN.xml`. Rate limit on /auth/register is 8/600s — pacing matters if running iter-13 back-to-back with iter-12 milestone tests in the same window.
rca_of_the_issue: User's expectation: 'domain verified → emails land for ANY recipient'. Reality: Resend test-mode API keys can only send to the account owner's email regardless of domain verification status. Domain verification + LIVE API key → arbitrary recipients work. Current state: domain `alreadyherellc.com` is verified (sends FROM and TO that domain succeed), but API key appears to still be a test key (sends to other recipient domains rejected with the 'You can only send testing emails to your own email address' message). Mitigation: rotate to a LIVE Resend API key at the Resend dashboard. No TokenForge code change required.
